report.name: Assembly Cycle Time Distribution Analysis - Engine Block Line

chartData.Description: Exceptional performance cycles - analyze best practices for replication
Optimal cycle time range - target performance for standardization
Good performance range within takt time requirements
Most frequent cycle time range - process mode performance
Peak frequency bin - average process performance level
Acceptable performance but approaching takt time limit
Slower cycles requiring root cause analysis
Above takt time - production capacity constraint identified
Significant delays - investigate special causes and bottlenecks

highlightsData.title: Takt Time Performance Analysis
highlightsData.content: With customer demand requiring 28.0 minutes per unit, 23% of cycles exceed takt time, creating production bottlenecks. The histogram reveals opportunity to improve capacity by reducing cycle time variation and eliminating delays above 27 minutes.

attachments.name: takt_time_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Comprehensive takt time vs cycle time performance analysis

highlightsData.title: Process Variation Reduction Opportunity
highlightsData.content: Current cycle time standard deviation of 3.2 minutes represents significant process variation. Industry best practices suggest potential for 40% variation reduction through standardized work implementation and bottleneck elimination.

attachments.name: variation_reduction_study.jpg
attachments.description: Process variation analysis and reduction opportunities study

highlightsData.title: Best Practice Identification
highlightsData.content: The 10 cycles completed in under 21 minutes demonstrate achievable performance levels. Video analysis reveals consistent application of lean techniques including pre-positioning, parallel operations, and smooth material flow.

attachments.name: best_practice_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Best practice cycle time analysis and standardization opportunities

highlightsData.title: Bottleneck Impact Assessment
highlightsData.content: Detailed time study reveals three primary bottlenecks: material handling delays (2.1 min average), equipment setup variations (1.8 min), and quality inspection inconsistencies (1.4 min). Addressing these could reduce mean cycle time by 18%.

attachments.name: bottleneck_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Comprehensive bottleneck identification and impact analysis

actionItems.title: Implement Standardized Work Instructions for Top Performers
actionItems.description: Document and standardize the methods used during the fastest cycle times (18-21 minutes). Create visual work instructions and train all operators on best practice techniques.

attachments.name: standardized_work_template.jpg
attachments.description: Standardized work instruction template for engine block assembly

actionItems.title: Optimize Material Handling and Presentation
actionItems.description: Redesign material presentation to eliminate walking and searching. Implement point-of-use storage, pre-kitting, and ergonomic workstation layout to reduce material handling time by 2+ minutes per cycle.

attachments.name: material_flow_redesign.jpg
attachments.description: Material handling optimization and workstation redesign plan

actionItems.title: Deploy Real-Time Cycle Time Monitoring System
actionItems.description: Install digital counters and displays to provide real-time cycle time feedback to operators. Include visual indicators for takt time compliance and performance trending.

attachments.name: monitoring_system_design.jpg
attachments.description: Real-time cycle time monitoring system architecture

actionItems.title: Conduct Equipment Setup Time Reduction Study
actionItems.description: Implement SMED (Single Minute Exchange of Die) methodology to reduce equipment changeover and setup variations. Target 50% reduction in setup-related cycle time delays.

attachments.name: smed_implementation_plan.jpg
attachments.description: SMED methodology implementation plan for setup time reduction

actionItems.title: Establish Cycle Time Performance Recognition Program
actionItems.description: Create team-based recognition system for achieving consistent cycle time targets. Include daily performance tracking, weekly team competitions, and monthly excellence awards.
